## Task 8: Test corpus YAML fixture

**Files:**
- Create: `tests/fixtures/matcher_corpus.yaml` (~246 cases)

This task is **data-heavy**. The fixture is the matcher's spec; budget time accordingly. Sample structure below — engineer drafts the full ~246 cases by referencing actual seeded customers/sites/products from `seed/customers.py`, `seed/sites.py`, `seed/products.py`, `seed/services.py`.

- [ ] **Step 8.1 — Read the seed data to identify real customer names + sites + SKUs**

```bash
python -c "
from db.session import session_scope
from sqlalchemy import text
with session_scope() as db:
    print('--- customers ---')
    for r in db.execute(text('SELECT name, aliases FROM customers ORDER BY name')).all():
        print(r.name, r.aliases or '')
    print('--- sites ---')
    for r in db.execute(text(
      'SELECT c.name AS customer, s.name AS site FROM customer_sites s '
      'JOIN customers c ON c.id = s.customer_id ORDER BY c.name'
    )).all():
        print(r.customer, '|', r.site)
    print('--- products (first 20) ---')
    for r in db.execute(text('SELECT sku, name FROM products LIMIT 20')).all():
        print(r.sku, '|', r.name)
"
```

Save the output for reference while drafting the corpus.

- [ ] **Step 8.2 — Create `tests/fixtures/matcher_corpus.yaml`**

Header + first-category template (rest follows the same pattern). The engineer fills in all 24 categories to hit the minimum case counts in the spec (Section: Test corpus + eval harness). Use real customer names from the seed output above.

```yaml
# Semantic-matcher eval corpus.
#
# Each row:
#   input: the user's free-text reference (typed or dictated)
#   expected_customer_name: canonical name from seed/customers.py (resolved
#     to UUID at test-load time) — OR null when the case expects no resolution
#   expected_alternatives_min: optional; for ambiguous/disambig cases that
#     should return null but surface multiple alternatives
#   category: one of the 24 spec categories
#   context: optional ConversationContext fields (active_account_name,
#     last_3_customer_names)
#
# Categories + minimum case counts (per spec):
#   canonical              10
#   abbreviation           10
#   possessive              8
#   alias                  10
#   apostrophe_variant      6
#   site_reference         12
#   site_shortform          8
#   typo_resolvable        10
#   long_form_sentence     15
#   pronoun_resolution     10
#   cross_customer_collision  10
#   ambiguous_reference    10
#   negative_case          10
#   generic_words           8
#   compound_reference      8
#   casual_colloquial       8
#   partial_match_noise     6
#   sku_canonical_code     10
#   sku_product_name       10
#   sku_qty_plus_product   10
#   sku_service             6
#   sku_colloquial          8
#   sku_ambiguous           6
#   sku_negative            6
#
# Total: ~246 cases.

cases:
  # ----------- canonical (10) -----------
  - input: "O'Reilly Family Orchards"
    expected_customer_name: "O'Reilly Family Orchards"
    category: canonical
  - input: "Martinez & Sons Orchards"
    expected_customer_name: "Martinez & Sons Orchards"
    category: canonical
  # ... add 8 more from real seed names ...

  # ----------- abbreviation (10) -----------
  - input: "O'Reilly"
    expected_customer_name: "O'Reilly Family Orchards"
    category: abbreviation
  - input: "Martinez"
    expected_customer_name: "Martinez & Sons Orchards"
    category: abbreviation
  # ... add 8 more ...

  # ----------- possessive (8) -----------
  - input: "O'Reilly's"
    expected_customer_name: "O'Reilly Family Orchards"
    category: possessive
  # ... add 7 more ...

  # ----------- alias (10) -----------
  # Use real entries from customers.aliases ...

  # ----------- apostrophe_variant (6) -----------
  - input: "OReilly"
    expected_customer_name: "O'Reilly Family Orchards"
    category: apostrophe_variant
  - input: "O Reilly"
    expected_customer_name: "O'Reilly Family Orchards"
    category: apostrophe_variant
  # ... add 4 more ...

  # ----------- site_reference (12) -----------
  # Use real site names from customer_sites — e.g. if O'Reilly has a
  # "back-40 yard" site, the case is { input: "the back-40", expected: O'Reilly }
  # ... fill in 12 cases ...

  # (continue for every category to the min count)
  # ===========================================
  # SKU CORPUS
  # ===========================================

  # ----------- sku_canonical_code (10) -----------
  - input: "PKG-BEES-3LB-ITA"
    expected_sku_kind: product
    expected_sku_code: "PKG-BEES-3LB-ITA"
    category: sku_canonical_code
  # ... 9 more SKU codes from seed/products.py + seed/services.py ...

  # ----------- sku_negative (6) -----------
  - input: "varroa swatter"
    expected_sku_kind: null
    category: sku_negative
  # ... 5 more ...
```

- [ ] **Step 8.3 — Validate the YAML loads + every customer name resolves to a real seed row**

Run a quick validation script (one-off; not committed):

```python
import yaml
from db.session import session_scope
from sqlalchemy import text

corpus = yaml.safe_load(open("tests/fixtures/matcher_corpus.yaml"))
with session_scope() as db:
    customer_names = {r.name for r in db.execute(text("SELECT name FROM customers"))}
    product_skus = {r.sku for r in db.execute(text("SELECT sku FROM products"))}
    service_skus = {r.sku for r in db.execute(text("SELECT sku FROM services"))}

missing_customers, missing_skus = [], []
for case in corpus["cases"]:
    if case.get("expected_customer_name") and case["expected_customer_name"] not in customer_names:
        missing_customers.append(case)
    if case.get("expected_sku_code") and case["expected_sku_code"] not in product_skus | service_skus:
        missing_skus.append(case)

print(f"Missing customers: {len(missing_customers)}")
print(f"Missing SKUs: {len(missing_skus)}")
for m in missing_customers[:5]: print(m)
```

Iterate until both lists are empty.

- [ ] **Step 8.4 — Subagent verification (mandatory per `feedback_real_corpus_tests_subagent_verified.md`)**

Dispatch an Explore subagent with this prompt:

```
You are reviewing a test corpus for an LLM-based customer/SKU matcher
in a commercial beekeeping CRM. The matcher needs to resolve
field-rep speech (dictated transcripts + typed messages) to customer
rows or product/service rows.

Files to read:
  - tests/fixtures/matcher_corpus.yaml — the corpus
  - seed/customers.py and seed/sites.py — real customer data
  - seed/products.py and seed/services.py — real SKU data
  - docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-12-semantic-matcher-design.md
    (Test corpus + eval harness section) — the design

Report:
  (a) Categories of variation present in real field-rep speech but
      MISSING from the corpus. Be specific (e.g. "regional accents",
      "company names with international characters", "site names
      starting with numbers"). Suggest 3-5 concrete new cases per gap.
  (b) Cases that are filler / tautological / too easy. Quote them.
  (c) Cases that reference customers, sites, products, or services
      NOT present in the seed data. Cite the case + the expected
      name. (False-positive failures.)
  (d) Whether each category threshold (in the spec) is calibrated
      correctly. Too lax = regressions slip through; too strict =
      false-positive CI failures. Recommend adjustments.
  (e) Cross-customer collisions: confirm there are real cross-customer
      collisions in the corpus (two customers sharing a token). If not,
      identify which seed customers should be in collision cases.

Return a structured Markdown report.
```

Save the report to `.reports/matcher_corpus_review.md`. Iterate the corpus based on findings. **Re-dispatch the subagent after each iteration until the report shows no critical gaps.**

- [ ] **Step 8.5 — Commit**

```bash
git add tests/fixtures/matcher_corpus.yaml .reports/matcher_corpus_review.md
git commit -m "feat(matcher): test corpus YAML — 246 real-text cases across 24 categories"
```

## Task 9: Eval harness

aiTitle: Record session logs for TRACE comparison

content.text: Task 8 is the data-heavy ~246-case corpus. This needs careful drafting from REAL seed data. Dispatching with strong constraints.

input.description: Build Task 8 corpus
input.prompt:
You are building Task 8 (Test corpus YAML fixture) for the semantic-matcher feature in the Waggle project. This is a data-heavy task — the corpus IS the matcher's behavioral spec.

## Working directory

`/Users/echoes/Documents/Contract/agentic-mcp-crm/waggle`

## Branch state

- HEAD: `471e03c` (Task 7 complete; backfill + boot reindex ready)
- DB is seeded; `customer_search_entries` + `sku_search_entries` populated
- ⚠️ Working tree has unrelated gmail-fixture mods — IGNORE

## What you must build

A single YAML file at `tests/fixtures/matcher_corpus.yaml` with approximately 246 real-text test cases across 24 categories. Each case maps a free-text input to an expected customer or SKU resolution.

## Category requirements (minimum case counts per spec)

| Category | Min cases |
|---|---|
| canonical | 10 |
| abbreviation | 10 |
| possessive | 8 |
| alias | 10 |
| apostrophe_variant | 6 |
| site_reference | 12 |
| site_shortform | 8 |
| typo_resolvable | 10 |
| long_form_sentence | 15 |
| pronoun_resolution | 10 |
| cross_customer_collision | 10 |
| ambiguous_reference | 10 |
| negative_case | 10 |
| generic_words | 8 |
| compound_reference | 8 |
| casual_colloquial | 8 |
| partial_match_noise | 6 |
| sku_canonical_code | 10 |
| sku_product_name | 10 |
| sku_qty_plus_product | 10 |
| sku_service | 6 |
| sku_colloquial | 8 |
| sku_ambiguous | 6 |
| sku_negative | 6 |

Total: ~196 cases minimum; aim for ~246 by exceeding minimums where natural variation exists.

## Process — DO IN ORDER

### Step 1 — DUMP THE REAL SEED DATA

Run this command (with TEST_DATABASE_URL set against the test DB, OR using the dev DB at localhost:5433):

```bash
DATABASE_URL="postgresql://agentic_mcp_crm:agentic_mcp_crm@localhost:5433/agentic_mcp_crm" python -c "
from db.session import session_scope
from sqlalchemy import text
with session_scope() as db:
    print('=== CUSTOMERS ===')
    for r in db.execute(text('SELECT name, aliases FROM customers ORDER BY name')).all():
        print(f'  {r.name} | aliases={r.aliases or []}')
    print('=== SITES ===')
    for r in db.execute(text(
        'SELECT c.name AS customer, s.name AS site FROM customer_sites s '
        'JOIN customers c ON c.id = s.customer_id ORDER BY c.name, s.name'
    )).all():
        print(f'  {r.customer} | {r.site}')
    print('=== PRODUCTS ===')
    for r in db.execute(text('SELECT sku, name FROM products ORDER BY sku')).all():
        print(f'  {r.sku} | {r.name}')
    print('=== SERVICES ===')
    for r in db.execute(text('SELECT sku, name FROM services ORDER BY sku')).all():
        print(f'  {r.sku} | {r.name}')
"
```

⚠️ The command might fail if `DATABASE_URL` env-var isn't set in the shell. If so, prepend the export. CAPTURE the full output — you'll cross-reference it for every case in the corpus.

### Step 2 — DRAFT THE CORPUS

Create `tests/fixtures/matcher_corpus.yaml`. Use this structure:

```yaml
# Semantic-matcher eval corpus.
#
# Each case:
#   input: the user's free-text reference (typed or dictated)
#   expected_customer_name: canonical name from seed/customers.py — OR null
#   expected_alternatives_min: optional, int — for ambiguous cases
#   expected_sku_kind: "product" | "service" | null — SKU cases only
#   expected_sku_code: SKU code string — SKU cases only
#   category: one of the 24 spec categories
#   context: optional dict with active_account_name, last_3_customer_names
#
# Categories and minimum case counts: [see prompt]
# Total: ~246 cases.

cases:
  # ----------- canonical (10) -----------
  - input: "O'Reilly Family Orchards"
    expected_customer_name: "O'Reilly Family Orchards"
    category: canonical
  # ... 9 more from REAL seeded customer names ...

  # ----------- abbreviation (10) -----------
  - input: "O'Reilly"
    expected_customer_name: "O'Reilly Family Orchards"
    category: abbreviation
  # ... 9 more, last-name or family-name only ...
```

## Rules for case construction

1. **ONLY use REAL data from the seed.** Every `expected_customer_name` MUST match a customer.name in the DB. Every `expected_sku_code` MUST match a real product.sku or service.sku. If you invent a name, the validation step in Step 3 will fail.

2. **Category definitions:**
   - `canonical` — the input IS the exact canonical name (matches `customers.name` verbatim)
   - `abbreviation` — input is a substring of the canonical name (e.g., last name only: "Martinez" → "Martinez & Sons Orchards")
   - `possessive` — input ends in 's or 's (e.g., "O'Reilly's" → "O'Reilly Family Orchards")
   - `alias` — input matches an entry in `customers.aliases` (use real aliases from the dump)
   - `apostrophe_variant` — input is the canonical name with apostrophes stripped/inserted (e.g., "OReilly" or "O Reilly" → "O'Reilly Family Orchards")
   - `site_reference` — input is a site name that uniquely identifies its customer
   - `site_shortform` — abbreviated/casual reference to a site (e.g., "the back-40" if a site is "Back-40 Yard")
   - `typo_resolvable` — common typo of canonical name (one-char swap, missing letter, etc.)
   - `long_form_sentence` — the customer reference embedded in a longer sentence (e.g., "Can you call O'Reilly about the hive count?")
   - `pronoun_resolution` — input uses "they", "them", "the customer" — REQUIRES `context.active_account_name`
   - `cross_customer_collision` — input is a token shared by 2+ customers; expected is the right one given context OR null with high alternatives count
   - `ambiguous_reference` — input matches multiple customers equally; expected is null with `expected_alternatives_min: 2+`
   - `negative_case` — input refers to a customer NOT in the seed; expected is `expected_customer_name: null`
   - `generic_words` — input is generic ("the orchard", "the apiary") that doesn't uniquely identify; expected null
   - `compound_reference` — input combines name + site/region (e.g., "Martinez in Modesto")
   - `casual_colloquial` — informal speech patterns ("the Martinez folks", "those guys at the Valley site")
   - `partial_match_noise` — input has noise tokens around the customer name
   - `sku_canonical_code` — input IS a real SKU code (uppercase, exact match)
   - `sku_product_name` — input matches a product.name
   - `sku_qty_plus_product` — input includes a quantity ("20 Italian queens")
   - `sku_service` — service code or service name (use real services)
   - `sku_colloquial` — informal product/service references ("varroa stuff", "queens 3lb")
   - `sku_ambiguous` — input matches multiple products/services equally
   - `sku_negative` — input refers to something NOT in products/services (expected_sku_kind: null)

3. **Cross-customer collisions:** look at the seed data dump and find customer names that share common tokens (e.g., multiple "Orchards", "Family", "Apiaries"). Build collision cases on those real tokens.

4. **Pronoun cases need context:**
   ```yaml
   - input: "What did they order last week?"
     expected_customer_name: "O'Reilly Family Orchards"
     category: pronoun_resolution
     context:
       active_account_name: "O'Reilly Family Orchards"
   ```

5. **Don't fill with tautological cases.** A case like `{ input: "Smith Apiaries", expected: "Smith Apiaries", category: canonical }` is good for canonical (verbatim match). But TWO such cases on the same customer is filler. Use different customers for each canonical case.

6. **YAML keys** must be exact: `input`, `expected_customer_name`, `expected_sku_kind`, `expected_sku_code`, `expected_alternatives_min`, `category`, `context`. Don't invent new keys.

### Step 3 — VALIDATE THE CORPUS LOADS + REFERENCES REAL DATA

Run this validation script:

```bash
DATABASE_URL="postgresql://agentic_mcp_crm:agentic_mcp_crm@localhost:5433/agentic_mcp_crm" python -c "
import yaml
from db.session import session_scope
from sqlalchemy import text

with open('tests/fixtures/matcher_corpus.yaml') as f:
    corpus = yaml.safe_load(f)

with session_scope() as db:
    customer_names = {r.name for r in db.execute(text('SELECT name FROM customers'))}
    product_skus = {r.sku for r in db.execute(text('SELECT sku FROM products'))}
    service_skus = {r.sku for r in db.execute(text('SELECT sku FROM services'))}

missing_customers, missing_skus = [], []
for i, case in enumerate(corpus['cases']):
    en = case.get('expected_customer_name')
    if en and en not in customer_names:
        missing_customers.append((i, case))
    sk = case.get('expected_sku_code')
    if sk and sk not in (product_skus | service_skus):
        missing_skus.append((i, case))

print(f'Total cases: {len(corpus[\"cases\"])}')

for idx, c in missing_skus[:10]:

# Category counts

cats = Counter(case['category'] for case in corpus['cases'])
print()
print('Category counts:')
for cat, n in sorted(cats.items()):
    print(f'  {cat}: {n}')
"
```

A single YAML file at `tests/fixtures/matcher_corpus.yaml` with approximately 246 real-text test cases across 24 categories. Each case maps a free-text input to an expected customer or SKU resolution.

## Category requirements (minimum case counts per spec)

| Category | Min cases |
|---|---|
| canonical | 10 |
| abbreviation | 10 |
| possessive | 8 |
| alias | 10 |
| apostrophe_variant | 6 |
| site_reference | 12 |
| site_shortform | 8 |
| typo_resolvable | 10 |
| long_form_sentence | 15 |
| pronoun_resolution | 10 |
| cross_customer_collision | 10 |
| ambiguous_reference | 10 |
| negative_case | 10 |
| generic_words | 8 |
| compound_reference | 8 |
| casual_colloquial | 8 |
| partial_match_noise | 6 |
| sku_canonical_code | 10 |
| sku_product_name | 10 |
| sku_qty_plus_product | 10 |

| sku_colloquial | 8 |
| sku_ambiguous | 6 |
| sku_negative | 6 |

Total: ~196 cases minimum; aim for ~246 by exceeding minimums where natural variation exists.

## Process — DO IN ORDER

### Step 1 — DUMP THE REAL SEED DATA

Run this command (with TEST_DATABASE_URL set against the test DB, OR using the dev DB at localhost:5433):

```bash
DATABASE_URL="postgresql://agentic_mcp_crm:agentic_mcp_crm@localhost:5433/agentic_mcp_crm" python -c "
from db.session import session_scope
from sqlalchemy import text
with session_scope() as db:
    print('=== CUSTOMERS ===')
    for r in db.execute(text('SELECT name, aliases FROM customers ORDER BY name')).all():
        print(f'  {r.name} | aliases={r.aliases or []}')
    print('=== SITES ===')
    for r in db.execute(text(
        'SELECT c.name AS customer, s.name AS site FROM customer_sites s '
        'JOIN customers c ON c.id = s.customer_id ORDER BY c.name, s.name'
    )).all():
        print(f'  {r.customer} | {r.site}')
    print('=== PRODUCTS ===')
    for r in db.execute(text('SELECT sku, name FROM products ORDER BY sku')).all():
        print(f'  {r.sku} | {r.name}')
    print('=== SERVICES ===')
    for r in db.execute(text('SELECT sku, name FROM services ORDER BY sku')).all():
        print(f'  {r.sku} | {r.name}')
"
```

⚠️ The command might fail if `DATABASE_URL` env-var isn't set in the shell. If so, prepend the export. CAPTURE the full output — you'll cross-reference it for every case in the corpus.

### Step 2 — DRAFT THE CORPUS

Create `tests/fixtures/matcher_corpus.yaml`. Use this structure:

```yaml
# Semantic-matcher eval corpus.
#
# Each case:
#   input: the user's free-text reference (typed or dictated)
#   expected_customer_name: canonical name from seed/customers.py — OR null
#   expected_alternatives_min: optional, int — for ambiguous cases
#   expected_sku_kind: "product" | "service" | null — SKU cases only
#   expected_sku_code: SKU code string — SKU cases only
#   category: one of the 24 spec categories
#   context: optional dict with active_account_name, last_3_customer_names
#
# Categories and minimum case counts: [see prompt]
# Total: ~246 cases.

cases:
  # ----------- canonical (10) -----------
  - input: "O'Reilly Family Orchards"
    expected_customer_name: "O'Reilly Family Orchards"
    category: canonical
  # ... 9 more from REAL seeded customer names ...

  # ----------- abbreviation (10) -----------
  - input: "O'Reilly"
    expected_customer_name: "O'Reilly Family Orchards"
    category: abbreviation
  # ... 9 more, last-name or family-name only ...
```

## Rules for case construction

1. **ONLY use REAL data from the seed.** Every `expected_customer_name` MUST match a customer.name in the DB. Every `expected_sku_code` MUST match a real product.sku or service.sku. If you invent a name, the validation step in Step 3 will fail.

2. **Category definitions:**
   - `canonical` — the input IS the exact canonical name (matches `customers.name` verbatim)
   - `abbreviation` — input is a substring of the canonical name (e.g., last name only: "Martinez" → "Martinez & Sons Orchards")
   - `possessive` — input ends in 's or 's (e.g., "O'Reilly's" → "O'Reilly Family Orchards")
   - `alias` — input matches an entry in `customers.aliases` (use real aliases from the dump)
   - `apostrophe_variant` — input is the canonical name with apostrophes stripped/inserted (e.g., "OReilly" or "O Reilly" → "O'Reilly Family Orchards")
   - `site_reference` — input is a site name that uniquely identifies its customer
   - `site_shortform` — abbreviated/casual reference to a site (e.g., "the back-40" if a site is "Back-40 Yard")
   - `typo_resolvable` — common typo of canonical name (one-char swap, missing letter, etc.)
   - `long_form_sentence` — the customer reference embedded in a longer sentence (e.g., "Can you call O'Reilly about the hive count?")
   - `pronoun_resolution` — input uses "they", "them", "the customer" — REQUIRES `context.active_account_name`
   - `cross_customer_collision` — input is a token shared by 2+ customers; expected is the right one given context OR null with high alternatives count
   - `ambiguous_reference` — input matches multiple customers equally; expected is null with `expected_alternatives_min: 2+`
   - `negative_case` — input refers to a customer NOT in the seed; expected is `expected_customer_name: null`
   - `generic_words` — input is generic ("the orchard", "the apiary") that doesn't uniquely identify; expected null
   - `compound_reference` — input combines name + site/region (e.g., "Martinez in Modesto")
   - `casual_colloquial` — informal speech patterns ("the Martinez folks", "those guys at the Valley site")
   - `partial_match_noise` — input has noise tokens around the customer name
   - `sku_canonical_code` — input IS a real SKU code (uppercase, exact match)
   - `sku_product_name` — input matches a product.name
   - `sku_qty_plus_product` — input includes a quantity ("20 Italian queens")
   - `sku_service` — service code or service name (use real services)
   - `sku_colloquial` — informal product/service references ("varroa stuff", "queens 3lb")
   - `sku_ambiguous` — input matches multiple products/services equally
   - `sku_negative` — input refers to something NOT in products/services (expected_sku_kind: null)

3. **Cross-customer collisions:** look at the seed data dump and find customer names that share common tokens (e.g., multiple "Orchards", "Family", "Apiaries"). Build collision cases on those real tokens.

4. **Pronoun cases need context:**
   ```yaml
   - input: "What did they order last week?"
     expected_customer_name: "O'Reilly Family Orchards"
     category: pronoun_resolution
     context:
       active_account_name: "O'Reilly Family Orchards"
   ```

5. **Don't fill with tautological cases.** A case like `{ input: "Smith Apiaries", expected: "Smith Apiaries", category: canonical }` is good for canonical (verbatim match). But TWO such cases on the same customer is filler. Use different customers for each canonical case.

6. **YAML keys** must be exact: `input`, `expected_customer_name`, `expected_sku_kind`, `expected_sku_code`, `expected_alternatives_min`, `category`, `context`. Don't invent new keys.

### Step 3 — VALIDATE THE CORPUS LOADS + REFERENCES REAL DATA

```bash
print(f'Total cases: {len(corpus[\"cases\"])}')

print(f'Missing SKUs: {len(missing_skus)}')
for idx, c in missing_customers[:10]:
    print(f'  case {idx}: input={c[\"input\"]!r} expected={c.get(\"expected_customer_name\")!r}')

# Category counts

cats = Counter(case['category'] for case in corpus['cases'])
print()
print('Category counts:')
for cat, n in sorted(cats.items()):
    print(f'  {cat}: {n}')
"
```

- `Missing customers: 0`
- `Missing SKUs: 0`
- Every category meets or exceeds its minimum count

### Step 4 — Commit the corpus

input.description: Verify Task 8 corpus thoroughness
input.prompt:
You are an independent reviewer of a test corpus for an LLM-based customer/SKU matcher in a commercial beekeeping CRM. This is a MANDATORY thoroughness review per the project's `feedback_real_corpus_tests_subagent_verified.md` rule and the matcher implementation plan's Step 8.4.

## Context

The matcher resolves field-rep speech (dictated transcripts + typed messages) to customer rows or product/service rows. The corpus tests across 24 categories. Each case has `input` + expected resolution (`expected_customer_name`, `expected_sku_kind`, `expected_sku_code`) + `category`.

## Files to read

- `/Users/echoes/Documents/Contract/agentic-mcp-crm/waggle/tests/fixtures/matcher_corpus.yaml` — the corpus (264 cases, 24 categories)
- `/Users/echoes/Documents/Contract/agentic-mcp-crm/waggle/seed/customers.py` — customer generation logic
- `/Users/echoes/Documents/Contract/agentic-mcp-crm/waggle/seed/sites.py` — site generation
- `/Users/echoes/Documents/Contract/agentic-mcp-crm/waggle/seed/products.py` — product generation
- `/Users/echoes/Documents/Contract/agentic-mcp-crm/waggle/seed/services.py` — service generation
- `/Users/echoes/Documents/Contract/agentic-mcp-crm/waggle/seed/faker_providers.py` (lines around `EDGE_CASE_CUSTOMERS`) — fixed customer names with aliases

You should ALSO query the actual seeded DB to compare what's in code vs what's actually in the rows. Use:
```bash
DATABASE_URL="postgresql://agentic_mcp_crm:agentic_mcp_crm@localhost:5433/agentic_mcp_crm" python -c "
from db.session import session_scope
from sqlalchemy import text
with session_scope() as db:
    for r in db.execute(text('SELECT name, aliases FROM customers ORDER BY name')).all():
        print(r.name, '|', r.aliases or [])
    print('---')
    for r in db.execute(text('SELECT c.name AS cust, s.name AS site FROM customer_sites s JOIN customers c ON c.id=s.customer_id ORDER BY c.name')).all():
        print(r.cust, '|', r.site)
    print('---')
    for r in db.execute(text('SELECT sku, name FROM products')).all():
        print(r.sku, '|', r.name)
"
```

## What to assess

**(a) Categories of variation present in real field-rep speech but MISSING from the corpus.**

Be specific. Examples of variation worth checking:
- Phonetic transcription artifacts (dictated speech often has missing/extra spaces, homophone errors)
- Regional/cultural name variants
- Numbers/years embedded in names ("Site 2024", "the new yard")
- Mid-sentence interruptions ("Talk to — what's the name — the Martinez guy")
- Mid-call corrections ("call Smith, no I mean Schmidt")
- Compound queries ("3 lb Italian queens for Martinez")
- Ambiguous SKU references with quantity ("twenty queens")
- Misspellings that look like other real customers
- Tone changes ("DEAR Mr. O'Reilly" vs "Hey OReilly")

Suggest 3-5 concrete new cases per identified gap.

**(b) Cases that are filler / tautological / too easy.**

A case like `{ input: "Smith Apiaries", expected: "Smith Apiaries", category: canonical }` is fine as one canonical example, but if there are MANY identical-shape cases varying only the customer name, that's filler. Quote specific cases that feel weak.

**(c) Cases that reference customers, sites, products, or services NOT present in the seed data.**

Cross-reference every `expected_customer_name` and `expected_sku_code` against the DB query output. If you find any, list them as false-positive failures.

**(d) Whether each category threshold is calibrated correctly.**

Categories with too few cases under-test the matcher. Categories with too many similar cases are filler. Look at the per-category counts (already in the corpus) and recommend any adjustments. Refer to the implementation plan's minimum counts:

| Category | Min |
|---|---|
| canonical | 10 |
| abbreviation | 10 |
| possessive | 8 |
| alias | 10 |
| apostrophe_variant | 6 |
| site_reference | 12 |
| site_shortform | 8 |
| typo_resolvable | 10 |
| long_form_sentence | 15 |
| pronoun_resolution | 10 |
| cross_customer_collision | 10 |
| ambiguous_reference | 10 |
| negative_case | 10 |
| generic_words | 8 |
| compound_reference | 8 |
| casual_colloquial | 8 |
| partial_match_noise | 6 |
| sku_canonical_code | 10 |
| sku_product_name | 10 |
| sku_qty_plus_product | 10 |
| sku_service | 6 |
| sku_colloquial | 8 |
| sku_ambiguous | 6 |
| sku_negative | 6 |

**(e) Cross-customer collisions.**

The implementer noted real collisions in the seed: "Mountain Valley" is an alias for both "Mountain Valley Farms" and "Mountain Valley Orchards"; "Blue Ridge" is alias for "Blue Ridge Family Orchards" and "Blue Ridge Farms"; "Haley" appears in 3 customer names. Confirm the corpus actually exercises these collisions — does it have cases where the input "Mountain Valley" is expected to surface BOTH alternatives (i.e., ambiguous_reference with `expected_alternatives_min: 2`)?

## Output

Write your full report to `/Users/echoes/Documents/Contract/agentic-mcp-crm/waggle/.reports/matcher_corpus_review.md`. Create the `.reports/` directory if needed.

The report should be structured Markdown with these sections:
- **Summary verdict:** APPROVED | NEEDS REVISIONS
- **(a) Missing variation categories** — list gaps + suggested cases
- **(b) Filler/tautological cases** — quote weak cases
- **(c) False-positive expected names** — cite cases referencing non-existent seed data
- **(d) Category threshold calibration** — per-category adjustments if any
- **(e) Cross-customer collision coverage** — verified or recommend more

Then ALSO return the same content (or a summary if it's very long) in your final report-back message to the controller. The file is for posterity; the message is for the controller's decision.

## Important

- Be specific. Generic complaints ("more diversity needed") aren't actionable. Quote actual cases. Suggest concrete additions.
- Don't grade leniently. The corpus IS the matcher's spec. If something is weak, say so.
- Don't grade harshly either. The corpus has 264 cases across 24 categories with real seed data. If it's good, say "approved."
- Don't modify the corpus yourself — that's the controller's job after reviewing your report.

## Self-Review

- Did I read the actual corpus AND the actual seed?
- Did I cross-reference each expected name against the DB?
- Did I give CONCRETE suggested cases for each identified gap, not just "add more X"?
- Did I write the report to disk AND summarize in my response?

## Report format

In your response to the controller:
- One paragraph summary
- Verdict (APPROVED | NEEDS REVISIONS)
- Top 3-5 most important findings (with case citations)
- Path to full report on disk
- Total cases reviewed

content.text:
Here is the review summary for the controller:

---

**VERDICT: NEEDS REVISIONS**

The corpus is structurally solid — all 264 cases across 24 categories meet or exceed their minimums, and every single expected customer name (36 distinct) and SKU code (23 distinct) was verified against the live seed database with zero false-positives. No invented data. However, there are specific correctness bugs and coverage gaps that need fixing before this corpus is ready to be the matcher's spec.

**Top 5 most important findings:**

1. **Incorrect alias case for "Blue Ridge" (line 158, `alias` category).** The DB records `"Blue Ridge"` as an explicit alias for `Blue Ridge Farms` only — it is not a shared alias. Treating it as a two-alternative ambiguous case in `alias` is wrong. The case should be `"Blue Ridge Family"` → `Blue Ridge Family Orchards` (the actual alias). The `cross_customer_collision` entry for bare `"Blue Ridge"` at line 479 is correct and should remain.

2. **Exact duplicate input `"O'Reilly west block"` in both `site_shortform` (line 281) and `compound_reference` (line 661).** The string is character-for-character identical. One instance should be differentiated (e.g., `"O'Reilly's west block"` with the possessive).

3. **`"varroa treatment" → TRT-OXALIC-R` in `sku_colloquial` is semantically wrong.** Both `TRT-OXALIC-R` (oxalic acid) and `TRT-FORMIC-R` (formic acid) are varroa treatments in the DB. A correct matcher will surface both. This case must move to `sku_ambiguous` with `expected_alternatives_min: 2`.

4. **Four seeded customers are never exercised as a direct resolution target:** `Blue Ridge Farms`, `Mesa Orchard Co.`, `Mountain Comb Wholesale`, and `Mountain Valley Orchards`. Each appears only as an implicit collision partner. The matcher can silently fail to resolve any of them without a test catching it. Four canonical or abbreviation cases are needed.

toolUseResult.prompt:
You are an independent reviewer of a test corpus for an LLM-based customer/SKU matcher in a commercial beekeeping CRM. This is a MANDATORY thoroughness review per the project's `feedback_real_corpus_tests_subagent_verified.md` rule and the matcher implementation plan's Step 8.4.

## Context

The matcher resolves field-rep speech (dictated transcripts + typed messages) to customer rows or product/service rows. The corpus tests across 24 categories. Each case has `input` + expected resolution (`expected_customer_name`, `expected_sku_kind`, `expected_sku_code`) + `category`.

## Files to read

- `/Users/echoes/Documents/Contract/agentic-mcp-crm/waggle/tests/fixtures/matcher_corpus.yaml` — the corpus (264 cases, 24 categories)
- `/Users/echoes/Documents/Contract/agentic-mcp-crm/waggle/seed/customers.py` — customer generation logic
- `/Users/echoes/Documents/Contract/agentic-mcp-crm/waggle/seed/sites.py` — site generation
- `/Users/echoes/Documents/Contract/agentic-mcp-crm/waggle/seed/products.py` — product generation
- `/Users/echoes/Documents/Contract/agentic-mcp-crm/waggle/seed/services.py` — service generation
- `/Users/echoes/Documents/Contract/agentic-mcp-crm/waggle/seed/faker_providers.py` (lines around `EDGE_CASE_CUSTOMERS`) — fixed customer names with aliases

You should ALSO query the actual seeded DB to compare what's in code vs what's actually in the rows. Use:
```bash
DATABASE_URL="postgresql://agentic_mcp_crm:agentic_mcp_crm@localhost:5433/agentic_mcp_crm" python -c "
from db.session import session_scope
from sqlalchemy import text
with session_scope() as db:
    for r in db.execute(text('SELECT name, aliases FROM customers ORDER BY name')).all():
        print(r.name, '|', r.aliases or [])
    print('---')
    for r in db.execute(text('SELECT c.name AS cust, s.name AS site FROM customer_sites s JOIN customers c ON c.id=s.customer_id ORDER BY c.name')).all():
        print(r.cust, '|', r.site)
    print('---')
    for r in db.execute(text('SELECT sku, name FROM products')).all():
        print(r.sku, '|', r.name)
"
```

## What to assess

**(a) Categories of variation present in real field-rep speech but MISSING from the corpus.**

Be specific. Examples of variation worth checking:
- Phonetic transcription artifacts (dictated speech often has missing/extra spaces, homophone errors)
- Regional/cultural name variants
- Numbers/years embedded in names ("Site 2024", "the new yard")
- Mid-sentence interruptions ("Talk to — what's the name — the Martinez guy")
- Mid-call corrections ("call Smith, no I mean Schmidt")
- Compound queries ("3 lb Italian queens for Martinez")
- Ambiguous SKU references with quantity ("twenty queens")
- Misspellings that look like other real customers
- Tone changes ("DEAR Mr. O'Reilly" vs "Hey OReilly")

Suggest 3-5 concrete new cases per identified gap.

**(b) Cases that are filler / tautological / too easy.**

A case like `{ input: "Smith Apiaries", expected: "Smith Apiaries", category: canonical }` is fine as one canonical example, but if there are MANY identical-shape cases varying only the customer name, that's filler. Quote specific cases that feel weak.

**(c) Cases that reference customers, sites, products, or services NOT present in the seed data.**

Cross-reference every `expected_customer_name` and `expected_sku_code` against the DB query output. If you find any, list them as false-positive failures.

**(d) Whether each category threshold is calibrated correctly.**

Categories with too few cases under-test the matcher. Categories with too many similar cases are filler. Look at the per-category counts (already in the corpus) and recommend any adjustments. Refer to the implementation plan's minimum counts:

| Category | Min |
|---|---|
| canonical | 10 |
| abbreviation | 10 |
| possessive | 8 |
| alias | 10 |
| apostrophe_variant | 6 |
| site_reference | 12 |
| site_shortform | 8 |
| typo_resolvable | 10 |
| long_form_sentence | 15 |
| pronoun_resolution | 10 |
| cross_customer_collision | 10 |
| ambiguous_reference | 10 |
| negative_case | 10 |
| generic_words | 8 |
| compound_reference | 8 |
| casual_colloquial | 8 |
| partial_match_noise | 6 |
| sku_canonical_code | 10 |
| sku_product_name | 10 |
| sku_qty_plus_product | 10 |
| sku_service | 6 |
| sku_colloquial | 8 |
| sku_ambiguous | 6 |
| sku_negative | 6 |

**(e) Cross-customer collisions.**

The implementer noted real collisions in the seed: "Mountain Valley" is an alias for both "Mountain Valley Farms" and "Mountain Valley Orchards"; "Blue Ridge" is alias for "Blue Ridge Family Orchards" and "Blue Ridge Farms"; "Haley" appears in 3 customer names. Confirm the corpus actually exercises these collisions — does it have cases where the input "Mountain Valley" is expected to surface BOTH alternatives (i.e., ambiguous_reference with `expected_alternatives_min: 2`)?

## Output

Write your full report to `/Users/echoes/Documents/Contract/agentic-mcp-crm/waggle/.reports/matcher_corpus_review.md`. Create the `.reports/` directory if needed.

The report should be structured Markdown with these sections:
- **Summary verdict:** APPROVED | NEEDS REVISIONS
- **(a) Missing variation categories** — list gaps + suggested cases
- **(b) Filler/tautological cases** — quote weak cases
- **(c) False-positive expected names** — cite cases referencing non-existent seed data
- **(d) Category threshold calibration** — per-category adjustments if any
- **(e) Cross-customer collision coverage** — verified or recommend more

Then ALSO return the same content (or a summary if it's very long) in your final report-back message to the controller. The file is for posterity; the message is for the controller's decision.

## Important

- Be specific. Generic complaints ("more diversity needed") aren't actionable. Quote actual cases. Suggest concrete additions.
- Don't grade leniently. The corpus IS the matcher's spec. If something is weak, say so.
- Don't grade harshly either. The corpus has 264 cases across 24 categories with real seed data. If it's good, say "approved."
- Don't modify the corpus yourself — that's the controller's job after reviewing your report.

## Self-Review

- Did I read the actual corpus AND the actual seed?
- Did I cross-reference each expected name against the DB?
- Did I give CONCRETE suggested cases for each identified gap, not just "add more X"?
- Did I write the report to disk AND summarize in my response?

## Report format

In your response to the controller:
- One paragraph summary
- Verdict (APPROVED | NEEDS REVISIONS)
- Top 3-5 most important findings (with case citations)
- Path to full report on disk
- Total cases reviewed

content.text:
Here is the review summary for the controller:

---

**VERDICT: NEEDS REVISIONS**

The corpus is structurally solid — all 264 cases across 24 categories meet or exceed their minimums, and every single expected customer name (36 distinct) and SKU code (23 distinct) was verified against the live seed database with zero false-positives. No invented data. However, there are specific correctness bugs and coverage gaps that need fixing before this corpus is ready to be the matcher's spec.

**Top 5 most important findings:**

1. **Incorrect alias case for "Blue Ridge" (line 158, `alias` category).** The DB records `"Blue Ridge"` as an explicit alias for `Blue Ridge Farms` only — it is not a shared alias. Treating it as a two-alternative ambiguous case in `alias` is wrong. The case should be `"Blue Ridge Family"` → `Blue Ridge Family Orchards` (the actual alias). The `cross_customer_collision` entry for bare `"Blue Ridge"` at line 479 is correct and should remain.

2. **Exact duplicate input `"O'Reilly west block"` in both `site_shortform` (line 281) and `compound_reference` (line 661).** The string is character-for-character identical. One instance should be differentiated (e.g., `"O'Reilly's west block"` with the possessive).

3. **`"varroa treatment" → TRT-OXALIC-R` in `sku_colloquial` is semantically wrong.** Both `TRT-OXALIC-R` (oxalic acid) and `TRT-FORMIC-R` (formic acid) are varroa treatments in the DB. A correct matcher will surface both. This case must move to `sku_ambiguous` with `expected_alternatives_min: 2`.

4. **Four seeded customers are never exercised as a direct resolution target:** `Blue Ridge Farms`, `Mesa Orchard Co.`, `Mountain Comb Wholesale`, and `Mountain Valley Orchards`. Each appears only as an implicit collision partner. The matcher can silently fail to resolve any of them without a test catching it. Four canonical or abbreviation cases are needed.

5. **Zero coverage for dictated-speech self-corrections and combined customer+SKU utterances.** The archetypal field-rep line — "20 Italian queens for Martinez" or "call — what's his name — the Martinez guy" — appears nowhere in the corpus. These are not exotic edge cases; they are the most common real-world input surface. At minimum 3–5 cases of each type should be added, folded into `long_form_sentence` and `casual_colloquial`.

**Additional finding (calibration):** The honey varietal category is heavily under-tested. The corpus covers 3 of 20 honey SKUs; the remaining 17 (sage, buckwheat, tupelo, alfalfa, sourwood, macadamia-blossom, manuka, Grade B drums, raw-jar variants) are entirely absent. Three additional `sku_product_name` cases covering different varietal families are the minimum fix.

**Full report on disk:** `/Users/echoes/Documents/Contract/agentic-mcp-crm/waggle/.reports/matcher_corpus_review.md`  
**Total cases reviewed:** 264
Verification: **NEEDS REVISIONS**. 5 specific findings + 1 calibration gap. Dispatching fix subagent.

input.prompt:
You are fixing specific issues in the matcher test corpus at `/Users/echoes/Documents/Contract/agentic-mcp-crm/waggle/tests/fixtures/matcher_corpus.yaml`. Independent reviewer flagged 5 main findings + 1 calibration gap.

## Working directory

`/Users/echoes/Documents/Contract/agentic-mcp-crm/waggle`

## Branch state

- HEAD: `dceb966` (corpus initial draft, 264 cases)
- Full review report: `.reports/matcher_corpus_review.md`

## Fixes required

### Fix 1 — Correct the "Blue Ridge" alias case

`tests/fixtures/matcher_corpus.yaml` line ~158, `alias` category:

The case treats bare "Blue Ridge" as a two-alternative ambiguous resolution. But the DB shows "Blue Ridge" is an explicit alias ONLY for `Blue Ridge Farms` (not for `Blue Ridge Family Orchards`). The shared-stem collision is "Blue Ridge" without alias intent.

**Replacement:** Change the `alias` case input to "Blue Ridge Family" (which IS an actual alias for `Blue Ridge Family Orchards`):

```yaml
  - input: "Blue Ridge Family"
    expected_customer_name: "Blue Ridge Family Orchards"
    category: alias
```

Verify the result via the DB:
```bash
DATABASE_URL="postgresql://agentic_mcp_crm:agentic_mcp_crm@localhost:5433/agentic_mcp_crm" python -c "
from db.session import session_scope
from sqlalchemy import text
with session_scope() as db:
    for r in db.execute(text(\"SELECT name, aliases FROM customers WHERE name ILIKE '%Blue Ridge%'\")).all():
        print(r.name, '|', r.aliases)
"
```

The existing `cross_customer_collision` entry for bare "Blue Ridge" at ~line 479 is CORRECT — leave it alone.

### Fix 2 — De-duplicate "O'Reilly west block"

The string "O'Reilly west block" appears character-for-character identical in TWO places:
- `site_shortform` category around line 281
- `compound_reference` category around line 661

Change ONE of them to a possessive variant. Recommendation: change the `compound_reference` instance to:
```yaml
  - input: "O'Reilly's west block"
    expected_customer_name: "O'Reilly Family Orchards"
    category: compound_reference
```

(The site_shortform instance stays as "O'Reilly west block".)

### Fix 3 — Move "varroa treatment" from sku_colloquial to sku_ambiguous

Current case maps "varroa treatment" → `TRT-OXALIC-R`. But the DB has BOTH `TRT-OXALIC-R` (oxalic acid) AND `TRT-FORMIC-R` (formic acid) as varroa treatments. A correct matcher would surface both.

**Replacement:** Remove from `sku_colloquial`, add to `sku_ambiguous`:

```yaml
  - input: "varroa treatment"
    expected_sku_kind: null
    expected_alternatives_min: 2
    category: sku_ambiguous
```

(No `expected_sku_code` — it's null when no single resolution is right.)

### Fix 4 — Add direct resolution cases for 4 under-tested customers

These 4 customers appear only as collision partners, never as direct resolution targets:
- `Blue Ridge Farms`
- `Mesa Orchard Co.`
- `Mountain Comb Wholesale`
- `Mountain Valley Orchards`

Add 4 new cases — one canonical and one abbreviation each is fine. Examples:

```yaml
  - input: "Blue Ridge Farms"
    expected_customer_name: "Blue Ridge Farms"
    category: canonical

  - input: "Mesa Orchard Co."
    expected_customer_name: "Mesa Orchard Co."
    category: canonical

  - input: "Mountain Comb"
    expected_customer_name: "Mountain Comb Wholesale"
    category: abbreviation

  - input: "Mountain Valley Orchards"
    expected_customer_name: "Mountain Valley Orchards"
    category: canonical
```

Place each in the appropriate category section in the YAML (find the existing canonical/abbreviation block and append).

### Fix 5 — Add dictated-speech and combined customer+SKU utterances

Add these in the appropriate categories:

```yaml
  # In long_form_sentence:
  - input: "20 Italian queens for Martinez next week"
    expected_customer_name: "Martinez & Sons Orchards"
    category: long_form_sentence

  - input: "send the new bee feed to the O'Reilly back-40"
    expected_customer_name: "O'Reilly Family Orchards"
    category: long_form_sentence

  - input: "schedule a hive inspection for the Mountain Valley Farms next visit"
    expected_customer_name: "Mountain Valley Farms"
    category: long_form_sentence

  # In casual_colloquial (self-correction patterns):
  - input: "call Smith — no I mean Schmidt about the queens"
    expected_customer_name: null
    category: casual_colloquial

  - input: "talk to — what's the name — the Martinez guy"
    expected_customer_name: "Martinez & Sons Orchards"
    category: casual_colloquial

  - input: "the O'Reilly — well, O'Reilly Family — folks"
    expected_customer_name: "O'Reilly Family Orchards"
    category: casual_colloquial
```

For the "Smith / Schmidt" case: if no Smith or Schmidt is in the seed, `expected_customer_name: null` (negative case). If a Smith IS in the seed, the case becomes ambiguous — choose a phrasing that fits the seed.

Verify against the seed before adding — use the DB query from Step 1 or simply read `seed/faker_providers.py` for `EDGE_CASE_CUSTOMERS`.

### Fix 6 — Honey varietal coverage (3 new sku_product_name cases)

Currently 3 of 20 honey SKUs are covered. Add 3 more from different varietal families. Pull real product names from the seed:

```bash
DATABASE_URL="postgresql://agentic_mcp_crm:agentic_mcp_crm@localhost:5433/agentic_mcp_crm" python -c "
from db.session import session_scope
from sqlalchemy import text
with session_scope() as db:
    for r in db.execute(text(\"SELECT sku, name FROM products WHERE name ILIKE '%honey%' OR sku LIKE 'HNY-%' ORDER BY sku\")).all():
        print(r.sku, '|', r.name)
"
```

Pick 3 SKUs from different families (e.g., one buckwheat, one sage, one tupelo) and add:

```yaml
  - input: "<real product name from query above>"
    expected_sku_kind: product
    expected_sku_code: "<real SKU>"
    category: sku_product_name
```

## After fixing

1. Re-run the corpus validation script:
```bash
DATABASE_URL="postgresql://agentic_mcp_crm:agentic_mcp_crm@localhost:5433/agentic_mcp_crm" python -c "
import yaml
from db.session import session_scope
from sqlalchemy import text

with open('tests/fixtures/matcher_corpus.yaml') as f:
    corpus = yaml.safe_load(f)

with session_scope() as db:
    customer_names = {r.name for r in db.execute(text('SELECT name FROM customers'))}
    product_skus = {r.sku for r in db.execute(text('SELECT sku FROM products'))}
    service_skus = {r.sku for r in db.execute(text('SELECT sku FROM services'))}

missing_customers, missing_skus = [], []
for i, case in enumerate(corpus['cases']):
    en = case.get('expected_customer_name')
    if en and en not in customer_names:
        missing_customers.append((i, case))
    sk = case.get('expected_sku_code')
    if sk and sk not in (product_skus | service_skus):
        missing_skus.append((i, case))

print(f'Total cases: {len(corpus[\"cases\"])}')
print(f'Missing customers: {len(missing_customers)}')
print(f'Missing SKUs: {len(missing_skus)}')
for idx, c in missing_customers[:10]:
    print(f'  case {idx}: input={c[\"input\"]!r}')
for idx, c in missing_skus[:10]:
    print(f'  case {idx}: input={c[\"input\"]!r}')

from collections import Counter
cats = Counter(case['category'] for case in corpus['cases'])
print()
print('Category counts:')
for cat, n in sorted(cats.items()):
    print(f'  {cat}: {n}')
"
```

Expected: 0 missing customers, 0 missing SKUs, all category counts still meet minimums.

2. Commit:
```bash
git add tests/fixtures/matcher_corpus.yaml
git commit -m "fix(matcher): corpus revisions — correct Blue Ridge alias, dedupe O'Reilly west block, varroa treatment ambiguity, 4 untested customer targets, dictated speech + self-correction patterns, honey varietal coverage"
```

3. Report back:
- **Status:** DONE | DONE_WITH_CONCERNS | BLOCKED
- Each of Fixes 1-6 applied? ✅/❌
- Validation output (verbatim)
- New commit SHA
- Anything that didn't apply cleanly

Currently 3 of 20 honey SKUs are covered. Add 3 more from different varietal families. Pull real product names from the seed:

```bash
DATABASE_URL="postgresql://agentic_mcp_crm:agentic_mcp_crm@localhost:5433/agentic_mcp_crm" python -c "
from db.session import session_scope
from sqlalchemy import text
with session_scope() as db:
    for r in db.execute(text(\"SELECT sku, name FROM products WHERE name ILIKE '%honey%' OR sku LIKE 'HNY-%' ORDER BY sku\")).all():
        print(r.sku, '|', r.name)
"
```

Pick 3 SKUs from different families (e.g., one buckwheat, one sage, one tupelo) and add:

```yaml

    expected_sku_kind: product
    expected_sku_code: "<real SKU>"
    category: sku_product_name
```

## After fixing

1. Re-run the corpus validation script:
```bash

    product_skus = {r.sku for r in db.execute(text('SELECT sku FROM products'))}

missing_customers, missing_skus = [], []
for i, case in enumerate(corpus['cases']):

    if en and en not in customer_names:

    sk = case.get('expected_sku_code')
    if sk and sk not in (product_skus | service_skus):

print(f'Total cases: {len(corpus[\"cases\"])}')
print(f'Missing customers: {len(missing_customers)}')
print(f'Missing SKUs: {len(missing_skus)}')
for idx, c in missing_customers[:10]:
    print(f'  case {idx}: input={c[\"input\"]!r}')
for idx, c in missing_skus[:10]:

from collections import Counter
cats = Counter(case['category'] for case in corpus['cases'])

print('Category counts:')
for cat, n in sorted(cats.items()):

"
```

2697	def stratified_sample(cases: list[EvalCase], fraction: float, seed: int) -> list[EvalCase]:
2698	    """Per-category fraction sampling for sample-mode eval."""
2699	    rng = random.Random(seed)
2700	    by_cat: dict[str, list[EvalCase]] = {}
2701	    for c in cases:
2702	        by_cat.setdefault(c.category, []).append(c)
2703	    sampled: list[EvalCase] = []
2704	    for cat, items in by_cat.items():
2705	        n = max(1, int(round(len(items) * fraction)))
2706	        sampled.extend(rng.sample(items, n))
2707	    return sampled

2711	    """Returns (correct, reason). Correct iff matcher output matches expectation."""
2712	    if case.expected_customer_id is None:

2715	        return False, f"expected null, got {match.customer_id}"
2716	    if match.customer_id == case.expected_customer_id:
2717	        return True, ""
2718	    return False, (
2719	        f"expected {case.expected_customer_id}, "
2720	        f"got {match.customer_id} (conf={match.confidence:.2f})"
2721	    )

2748	    ]
2749	    for k, v in metadata.items():

2751	    lines.append("\n## Per-category results\n")

2753	    lines.append("|---|---|---|---|---|---|")
2754	    for cat, r in sorted(results.items()):
2755	        prec = (r.correct / r.total) if r.total else 0.0
2756	        thr = thresholds.get(cat, 0.0)
2757	        status = "PASS" if prec >= thr else "FAIL"
2758	        lines.append(
2759	            f"| {cat} | {r.total} | {r.correct} | {prec:.2%} | {thr:.2%} | {status} |"
2760	        )
2761	    lines.append("\n## Failures\n")
2762	    for cat, r in sorted(results.items()):
2763	        if not r.failures:
2764	            continue
2765	        lines.append(f"### {cat}\n")
2766	        for inp, why in r.failures[:10]:
2767	            lines.append(f"- `{inp}` — {why}")
2768	        if len(r.failures) > 10:
2769	            lines.append(f"- _(+ {len(r.failures) - 10} more)_")
2770	    path.write_text("\n".join(lines))

## Task 9: Eval harness

**Files:**
- Create: `tests/test_matcher_eval.py`
- Create: `tests/_eval_helpers.py` (corpus loader + metric computation + report writer)
- Modify: `.gitignore` (allow `.reports/` to be committed)

- [ ] **Step 9.1 — Write the eval-harness test scaffolding**

Create `tests/_eval_helpers.py`:

```python
"""Helpers for the matcher eval harness — corpus loading, metric
computation, report writing. Kept out of the test file so the test
module stays focused on assertions."""
# pyright: reportMissingImports=false
from __future__ import annotations

import hashlib
import os
import random
import subprocess
import uuid
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

import yaml
from sqlalchemy import text
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session


CORPUS_PATH = Path("tests/fixtures/matcher_corpus.yaml")
REPORTS_DIR = Path(".reports")

EVAL_MODE = os.environ.get("MATCHER_EVAL_MODE", "sample")  # 'sample' | 'full'
SAMPLE_FRACTION = 0.20
SAMPLE_SEED = 42

CUSTOMER_THRESHOLDS = {
    "canonical": 1.00,
    "abbreviation": 0.98,
    "possessive": 0.98,
    "alias": 0.98,
    "apostrophe_variant": 0.98,
    "site_reference": 0.95,
    "site_shortform": 0.95,
    "typo_resolvable": 0.90,
    "long_form_sentence": 0.95,
    "pronoun_resolution": 0.85,
    "cross_customer_collision": 0.90,
    "ambiguous_reference": 0.95,
    "negative_case": 1.00,
    "generic_words": 1.00,
    "compound_reference": 0.85,
    "casual_colloquial": 0.95,
    "partial_match_noise": 0.95,
}

SKU_THRESHOLDS = {
    "sku_canonical_code": 1.00,
    "sku_product_name": 0.98,
    "sku_qty_plus_product": 0.98,
    "sku_service": 0.95,
    "sku_colloquial": 0.90,
    "sku_ambiguous": 0.95,
    "sku_negative": 1.00,
}


@dataclass
class EvalCase:
    input: str
    category: str
    expected_customer_id: uuid.UUID | None = None
    expected_sku_id: uuid.UUID | None = None
    expected_sku_kind: str | None = None
    expected_alternatives_min: int = 0
    context_active_account_id: uuid.UUID | None = None
    context_last_3_customer_ids: list[uuid.UUID] = field(default_factory=list)


@dataclass
class CategoryResult:
    category: str
    total: int = 0
    correct: int = 0
    failures: list[tuple[str, str]] = field(default_factory=list)  # (input, why)


def load_corpus(*, kind: str, db: Session) -> list[EvalCase]:
    """Load + resolve symbolic names to UUIDs against the live DB."""
    raw = yaml.safe_load(CORPUS_PATH.read_text())
    customer_name_to_id: dict[str, uuid.UUID] = {
        r.name: r.id for r in db.execute(text("SELECT id, name FROM customers"))
    }
    product_sku_to_id: dict[str, uuid.UUID] = {
        r.sku: r.id for r in db.execute(text("SELECT id, sku FROM products"))
    }
    service_sku_to_id: dict[str, uuid.UUID] = {
        r.sku: r.id for r in db.execute(text("SELECT id, sku FROM services"))
    }

    cases: list[EvalCase] = []
    for c in raw["cases"]:
        category = c["category"]
        is_sku_category = category.startswith("sku_")
        if kind == "customer" and is_sku_category:
            continue
        if kind == "sku" and not is_sku_category:
            continue

        case = EvalCase(input=c["input"], category=category)

        if not is_sku_category:
            name = c.get("expected_customer_name")
            if name is not None:
                if name not in customer_name_to_id:
                    raise ValueError(
                        f"Corpus case references unknown customer {name!r}"
                    )
                case.expected_customer_id = customer_name_to_id[name]
        else:
            kind_val = c.get("expected_sku_kind")
            sku = c.get("expected_sku_code")
            if kind_val == "product" and sku:
                case.expected_sku_id = product_sku_to_id[sku]
                case.expected_sku_kind = "product"
            elif kind_val == "service" and sku:
                case.expected_sku_id = service_sku_to_id[sku]
                case.expected_sku_kind = "service"
            elif kind_val is None:
                pass  # negative case
            else:
                raise ValueError(f"Bad sku case shape: {c}")

        case.expected_alternatives_min = c.get("expected_alternatives_min", 0)

        ctx = c.get("context") or {}
        if "active_account_name" in ctx:
            name = ctx["active_account_name"]
            if name:
                case.context_active_account_id = customer_name_to_id.get(name)
        if "last_3_customer_names" in ctx:
            case.context_last_3_customer_ids = [
                customer_name_to_id[n] for n in ctx["last_3_customer_names"]
                if n in customer_name_to_id
            ]

        cases.append(case)
    return cases


def stratified_sample(cases: list[EvalCase], fraction: float, seed: int) -> list[EvalCase]:
    """Per-category fraction sampling for sample-mode eval."""
    rng = random.Random(seed)
    by_cat: dict[str, list[EvalCase]] = {}
    for c in cases:
        by_cat.setdefault(c.category, []).append(c)
    sampled: list[EvalCase] = []
    for cat, items in by_cat.items():
        n = max(1, int(round(len(items) * fraction)))
        sampled.extend(rng.sample(items, n))
    return sampled


def evaluate_case_customer(case: EvalCase, match: Any) -> tuple[bool, str]:
    """Returns (correct, reason). Correct iff matcher output matches expectation."""
    if case.expected_customer_id is None:
        if match.customer_id is None:
            return True, ""
        return False, f"expected null, got {match.customer_id}"
    if match.customer_id == case.expected_customer_id:
        return True, ""
    return False, (
        f"expected {case.expected_customer_id}, "
        f"got {match.customer_id} (conf={match.confidence:.2f})"
    )


def evaluate_case_sku(case: EvalCase, match: Any) -> tuple[bool, str]:
    if case.expected_sku_kind is None:
        if match.sku_kind is None:
            return True, ""
        return False, f"expected null, got {match.sku_kind}/{match.sku_code}"
    actual_id = match.product_id if match.sku_kind == "product" else match.service_id
    if actual_id == case.expected_sku_id and match.sku_kind == case.expected_sku_kind:
        return True, ""
    return False, (
        f"expected {case.expected_sku_kind}/{case.expected_sku_id}, "
        f"got {match.sku_kind}/{actual_id}"
    )


def write_report(
    *, kind: str, results: dict[str, CategoryResult],
    thresholds: dict[str, float], metadata: dict[str, Any],
) -> Path:
    REPORTS_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
    path = REPORTS_DIR / f"matcher_eval_{kind}.md"
    lines = [
        f"# Matcher eval report ({kind})\n",
        "## Metadata\n",
        "| Key | Value |", "|---|---|",
    ]
    for k, v in metadata.items():
        lines.append(f"| {k} | `{v}` |")
    lines.append("\n## Per-category results\n")
    lines.append("| Category | N | Correct | Precision | Threshold | Status |")
    lines.append("|---|---|---|---|---|---|")
    for cat, r in sorted(results.items()):
        prec = (r.correct / r.total) if r.total else 0.0
        thr = thresholds.get(cat, 0.0)
        status = "PASS" if prec >= thr else "FAIL"
        lines.append(
            f"| {cat} | {r.total} | {r.correct} | {prec:.2%} | {thr:.2%} | {status} |"
        )
    lines.append("\n## Failures\n")
    for cat, r in sorted(results.items()):
        if not r.failures:
            continue
        lines.append(f"### {cat}\n")
        for inp, why in r.failures[:10]:
            lines.append(f"- `{inp}` — {why}")
        if len(r.failures) > 10:
            lines.append(f"- _(+ {len(r.failures) - 10} more)_")
    path.write_text("\n".join(lines))
    return path


def resolve_git_sha() -> str:
    try:
        return subprocess.check_output(
            ["git", "rev-parse", "--short", "HEAD"], text=True
        ).strip()
    except Exception:
        return "unknown"


def corpus_sha() -> str:
    return hashlib.sha256(CORPUS_PATH.read_bytes()).hexdigest()[:12]
```

Create `tests/test_matcher_eval.py`:

```python
"""Eval harness: runs the matcher corpus and asserts per-category thresholds.

Modes:
    MATCHER_EVAL_MODE=sample (default) — 20% stratified sample per category
    MATCHER_EVAL_MODE=full              — every case
    MATCHER_EVAL_FULL=1 (env)           — equivalent to full
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import os
import uuid

import pytest

from agents.schemas import ConversationContext, SkuContext
from providers import semantic_matcher
from tests._eval_helpers import (
    CUSTOMER_THRESHOLDS, EVAL_MODE, SAMPLE_FRACTION, SAMPLE_SEED,
    SKU_THRESHOLDS, CategoryResult,
    corpus_sha, evaluate_case_customer, evaluate_case_sku,
    load_corpus, resolve_git_sha, stratified_sample, write_report,
)


require_openai_key = pytest.mark.skipif(
    not os.environ.get("OPENAI_API_KEY"),
    reason="OPENAI_API_KEY not set — eval skipped",
)


@require_openai_key
def test_matcher_eval_customer(db_session_with_seed):
    semantic_matcher.backfill_all(db_session_with_seed)
    cases = load_corpus(kind="customer", db=db_session_with_seed)
    if EVAL_MODE == "sample" and not os.environ.get("MATCHER_EVAL_FULL"):
        cases = stratified_sample(cases, SAMPLE_FRACTION, SAMPLE_SEED)

    results: dict[str, CategoryResult] = {}
    semantic_matcher.clear_caches()
    for case in cases:
        ctx = ConversationContext(
            active_account_id=case.context_active_account_id,
            last_3_customer_ids=case.context_last_3_customer_ids,
        )
        match = semantic_matcher.match_customer(
            case.input, conversation_ctx=ctx,
            conversation_id=uuid.uuid4(),
            db=db_session_with_seed,
        )
        result = results.setdefault(case.category, CategoryResult(case.category))
        correct, why = evaluate_case_customer(case, match)
        result.total += 1
        if correct:
            result.correct += 1
        else:
            result.failures.append((case.input, why))

    from app.config import get_settings
    settings = get_settings()
    metadata = {
        "embedding_model": "all-MiniLM-L6-v2",
        "disambig_model": settings.copilot_matcher_disambig_model,
        "prompt_version": semantic_matcher.PROMPT_VERSION,
        "corpus_sha": corpus_sha(),
        "git_sha": resolve_git_sha(),
        "eval_mode": EVAL_MODE if not os.environ.get("MATCHER_EVAL_FULL") else "full",
    }
    write_report(
        kind="customer", results=results,
        thresholds=CUSTOMER_THRESHOLDS, metadata=metadata,
    )

    failures: list[str] = []
    for cat, threshold in CUSTOMER_THRESHOLDS.items():
        r = results.get(cat)
        if not r or not r.total:
            continue  # sample mode may skip a category — accept
        prec = r.correct / r.total
        if prec < threshold:
            failures.append(f"{cat}: {prec:.2%} < {threshold:.2%}")
    assert not failures, "Threshold breaches:\n" + "\n".join(failures)


@require_openai_key
def test_matcher_eval_sku(db_session_with_seed):
    semantic_matcher.backfill_all(db_session_with_seed)
    cases = load_corpus(kind="sku", db=db_session_with_seed)
    if EVAL_MODE == "sample" and not os.environ.get("MATCHER_EVAL_FULL"):
        cases = stratified_sample(cases, SAMPLE_FRACTION, SAMPLE_SEED)

    results: dict[str, CategoryResult] = {}
    semantic_matcher.clear_caches()
    for case in cases:
        match = semantic_matcher.match_sku(
            case.input, sku_context=SkuContext(),
            conversation_id=uuid.uuid4(),
            db=db_session_with_seed,
        )
        result = results.setdefault(case.category, CategoryResult(case.category))
        correct, why = evaluate_case_sku(case, match)
        result.total += 1
        if correct:
            result.correct += 1
        else:
            result.failures.append((case.input, why))

    from app.config import get_settings
    settings = get_settings()
    metadata = {
        "embedding_model": "all-MiniLM-L6-v2",
        "disambig_model": settings.copilot_matcher_disambig_model,
        "prompt_version": semantic_matcher.PROMPT_VERSION,
        "corpus_sha": corpus_sha(),
        "git_sha": resolve_git_sha(),
        "eval_mode": EVAL_MODE if not os.environ.get("MATCHER_EVAL_FULL") else "full",
    }
    write_report(
        kind="sku", results=results,
        thresholds=SKU_THRESHOLDS, metadata=metadata,
    )

    failures: list[str] = []
    for cat, threshold in SKU_THRESHOLDS.items():
        r = results.get(cat)
        if not r or not r.total:
            continue
        prec = r.correct / r.total
        if prec < threshold:
            failures.append(f"{cat}: {prec:.2%} < {threshold:.2%}")
    assert not failures, "SKU threshold breaches:\n" + "\n".join(failures)
```

- [ ] **Step 9.2 — Make `.reports/` committable**

If `.gitignore` ignores `.reports/`, allow it:

If any threshold fails: inspect the report's "Failures" section, decide whether to (a) fix the prompt, (b) add an alias to a customer row, (c) adjust a corpus case that's miscategorized, or (d) loosen a threshold (last resort; never below 0.70 without commit-message justification). Re-run.

You are implementing Task 9 (Eval harness) for the semantic-matcher feature in the Waggle project. Builds the test harness that runs the corpus against the live matcher and asserts per-category precision thresholds.

## Working directory

`/Users/echoes/Documents/Contract/agentic-mcp-crm/waggle`

## Branch state

- HEAD: `db2a4b0` (Task 8 corpus complete, 277 cases)
- All matcher stages A/B/C + backfill + boot reindex implemented
- OPENAI_API_KEY in .env
- ⚠️ Working tree has unrelated gmail-fixture mods — IGNORE

## Task Description (full text from plan, lines 2543-2954)

[The plan's full code for `tests/_eval_helpers.py`, `tests/test_matcher_eval.py`, `.reports/README.md`, and the run commands.]

The plan specifies:
- `tests/_eval_helpers.py` — corpus loader, stratified sampler, evaluate functions, report writer, helpers (corpus_sha, resolve_git_sha)
- `tests/test_matcher_eval.py` — two tests (`test_matcher_eval_customer`, `test_matcher_eval_sku`), gated on OPENAI_API_KEY
- `.reports/README.md` — committed so directory exists
- `MATCHER_EVAL_MODE=sample` (default, 0.20 stratified) vs `MATCHER_EVAL_FULL=1`

**Key constants in `_eval_helpers.py`:**
- CORPUS_PATH = Path("tests/fixtures/matcher_corpus.yaml")
- REPORTS_DIR = Path(".reports")
- EVAL_MODE = os.environ.get("MATCHER_EVAL_MODE", "sample")
- SAMPLE_FRACTION = 0.20
- SAMPLE_SEED = 42
- CUSTOMER_THRESHOLDS dict (17 categories with values 0.85–1.00)
- SKU_THRESHOLDS dict (7 categories with values 0.90–1.00)

**Threshold table (from plan):**

| Category | Threshold |
|---|---|
| canonical | 1.00 |
| abbreviation | 0.98 |
| possessive | 0.98 |
| alias | 0.98 |
| apostrophe_variant | 0.98 |
| site_reference | 0.95 |
| site_shortform | 0.95 |
| typo_resolvable | 0.90 |
| long_form_sentence | 0.95 |
| pronoun_resolution | 0.85 |
| cross_customer_collision | 0.90 |
| ambiguous_reference | 0.95 |
| negative_case | 1.00 |
| generic_words | 1.00 |
| compound_reference | 0.85 |
| casual_colloquial | 0.95 |
| partial_match_noise | 0.95 |
| sku_canonical_code | 1.00 |
| sku_product_name | 0.98 |
| sku_qty_plus_product | 0.98 |
| sku_service | 0.95 |
| sku_colloquial | 0.90 |
| sku_ambiguous | 0.95 |
| sku_negative | 1.00 |

(Note: 'sku_ambiguous' and 'ambiguous_reference' have special handling — the plan's `evaluate_case_customer` and `evaluate_case_sku` only check primary match; alternatives count isn't currently in the eval logic. That's a known limitation acknowledged by the plan.)

## Process

### Step 9.1 — Create files

Create `tests/_eval_helpers.py` with the full content from the plan (the long code block I quoted above — paste it verbatim, then verify it imports cleanly).

Create `tests/test_matcher_eval.py` with the full content from the plan (the two test functions).

### Step 9.2 — Allow `.reports/` to be committed

```bash
mkdir -p .reports
echo "# Eval reports — committed so PR diffs show quality deltas" > .reports/README.md
```

Check `.gitignore` — if it ignores `.reports/`, add `!.reports/*.md` exception OR use `git add -f .reports/README.md` on commit.

### Step 9.3 — Run eval in sample mode

```bash
TEST_DATABASE_URL="postgresql://agentic_mcp_crm:agentic_mcp_crm@localhost:5433/agentic_mcp_crm_test" MATCHER_EVAL_MODE=sample pytest tests/test_matcher_eval.py -v
```

Expected outcome (one of):
- PASS — all thresholds met. Great.
- FAIL — some categories fall short. Document which categories + actual precision. DO NOT silently loosen thresholds. Report DONE_WITH_CONCERNS with the specific failures.

The eval will take 5-10 minutes (real LLM calls per case in sample mode = ~55 cases at ~10s each).

The reports go to `.reports/matcher_eval_customer.md` and `.reports/matcher_eval_sku.md`.

### Step 9.4 — DON'T run full mode this dispatch

Skip Step 9.4 from the plan. The controller will run full mode separately. Sample mode is sufficient for the implementer commit.

### Step 9.5 — Commit

```bash
git add tests/test_matcher_eval.py tests/_eval_helpers.py .reports/README.md .reports/matcher_eval_customer.md .reports/matcher_eval_sku.md
git commit -m "feat(matcher): eval harness with per-category thresholds + versioned reports"
```

## Project Context

**Pre-work:**
1. Read the plan's full code (already pasted above in mind) and copy it verbatim into the new files.
2. Check `.gitignore` for `.reports/` entries. The plan says to "make .reports/ committable" — verify nothing blocks the commit.
3. Verify `from agents.schemas import ConversationContext, SkuContext` works (they exist from Task 2).
4. Verify `semantic_matcher.PROMPT_VERSION` exists (it's a module constant from Task 4).
5. Verify `app.config.get_settings()` exists (used in metadata for reports).

**Project rules (.claude/rules/):**
- `code-quality.md` — pyright clean, docstrings, `from __future__ import annotations`
- `fail-loud.md` — `load_corpus` raises `ValueError` on unknown customer/SKU references. Don't catch.
- `e2e-testing.md` — real LLM, real DB. The tests use `@require_openai_key` skip — that's the only allowed dependency-skip pattern.

**Important notes:**

1. **PROMPT_VERSION reference:** `semantic_matcher.PROMPT_VERSION` is a module constant. Don't redefine it in the eval module.

2. **The eval can take 5-10 min:** real LLM calls per case. Use Bash with run_in_background=true if needed, OR just be patient with the foreground run.

3. **`db_session_with_seed` fixture exists in conftest** — it provides a seeded DB. The eval tests call `semantic_matcher.backfill_all(db_session_with_seed)` first to populate search entries.

4. **If a threshold fails:** don't loosen it. Document the failure. The controller decides what to do next.

5. **The `evaluate_case_customer` and `evaluate_case_sku` logic checks only primary match (customer_id / sku_kind), NOT alternatives counts.** For `ambiguous_reference` and `sku_ambiguous` categories, the expected value is `null` — so the matcher returns `customer_id=None` to be "correct." This means these categories test that the matcher CORRECTLY refuses to disambiguate when ambiguity is real.

## Your Job

1. Create `tests/_eval_helpers.py` with the plan's full content.
2. Create `tests/test_matcher_eval.py` with the plan's full content.
3. Create `.reports/README.md`.
4. Check + update `.gitignore` if needed.
5. Run pyright on the new files. Confirm `0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 informations` literally.
6. Run sample-mode eval. This will take time.
7. Capture and paste the full pytest output (including the threshold-check assertion at the end).
8. If thresholds pass: commit. If some fail: still commit the harness (it's the artifact) but report DONE_WITH_CONCERNS with the specific category failures.
9. Report back.

## Self-Review

- Are both threshold dicts (CUSTOMER_THRESHOLDS, SKU_THRESHOLDS) complete and correct?
- Does `load_corpus` correctly distinguish customer vs SKU cases by category prefix?
- Does `stratified_sample` produce per-category samples (not just a random 20%)?
- Does the test write reports before asserting (so reports exist even on failure)?
- Pyright clean?
- Did I commit BEFORE running into trouble (or after the eval completes)?

## Report Format

- **Status:** DONE | DONE_WITH_CONCERNS | BLOCKED | NEEDS_CONTEXT
- Files created
- Pytest output (full — paste verbatim if it fits, summarize if too long)
- For each failed category (if any): name + actual precision + threshold + sample failure inputs
- Reports written: confirmed exist at `.reports/matcher_eval_customer.md` and `.reports/matcher_eval_sku.md`
- Pyright output (verbatim)
- Commit SHA
- Self-review findings

DONE_WITH_CONCERNS if the harness is correct but some category precisions fall below threshold (that's expected and informative — the controller iterates). DONE only if everything passes. BLOCKED if the harness itself has issues (can't load corpus, can't write report, etc.).


1. Read the plan's full code (already pasted above in mind) and copy it verbatim into the new files.
2. Check `.gitignore` for `.reports/` entries. The plan says to "make .reports/ committable" — verify nothing blocks the commit.
3. Verify `from agents.schemas import ConversationContext, SkuContext` works (they exist from Task 2).
4. Verify `semantic_matcher.PROMPT_VERSION` exists (it's a module constant from Task 4).
5. Verify `app.config.get_settings()` exists (used in metadata for reports).

**Project rules (.claude/rules/):**
- `code-quality.md` — pyright clean, docstrings, `from __future__ import annotations`
- `fail-loud.md` — `load_corpus` raises `ValueError` on unknown customer/SKU references. Don't catch.
- `e2e-testing.md` — real LLM, real DB. The tests use `@require_openai_key` skip — that's the only allowed dependency-skip pattern.

**Important notes:**

1. **PROMPT_VERSION reference:** `semantic_matcher.PROMPT_VERSION` is a module constant. Don't redefine it in the eval module.

2. **The eval can take 5-10 min:** real LLM calls per case. Use Bash with run_in_background=true if needed, OR just be patient with the foreground run.

3. **`db_session_with_seed` fixture exists in conftest** — it provides a seeded DB. The eval tests call `semantic_matcher.backfill_all(db_session_with_seed)` first to populate search entries.

5. **The `evaluate_case_customer` and `evaluate_case_sku` logic checks only primary match (customer_id / sku_kind), NOT alternatives counts.** For `ambiguous_reference` and `sku_ambiguous` categories, the expected value is `null` — so the matcher returns `customer_id=None` to be "correct." This means these categories test that the matcher CORRECTLY refuses to disambiguate when ambiguity is real.

7. Capture and paste the full pytest output (including the threshold-check assertion at the end).
8. If thresholds pass: commit. If some fail: still commit the harness (it's the artifact) but report DONE_WITH_CONCERNS with the specific category failures.
9. Report back.

## Self-Review

- Did I commit BEFORE running into trouble (or after the eval completes)?

## Report Format

- **Status:** DONE | DONE_WITH_CONCERNS | BLOCKED | NEEDS_CONTEXT
- Files created
- Pytest output (full — paste verbatim if it fits, summarize if too long)
- For each failed category (if any): name + actual precision + threshold + sample failure inputs
- Reports written: confirmed exist at `.reports/matcher_eval_customer.md` and `.reports/matcher_eval_sku.md`
- Pyright output (verbatim)
- Commit SHA
- Self-review findings

DONE_WITH_CONCERNS if the harness is correct but some category precisions fall below threshold (that's expected and informative — the controller iterates). DONE only if everything passes. BLOCKED if the harness itself has issues (can't load corpus, can't write report, etc.).

input.content: Task 9 eval surfaced that current matcher implementation falls short of plan's aspirational thresholds: customer 77.1% overall vs ~95% target, SKU 83.3% vs ~95% target. Specific failure modes (from .reports/matcher_eval_customer.md): (1) ambiguous queries resolving to a specific customer when they should return null+alternatives — the LLM disambiguator is too eager; (2) pronoun resolution doesn't actually use active_account_id correctly in the prompt; (3) possessives/typos sometimes fail Stage A retrieval (FTS tokenization misses apostrophe variants); (4) SKU qty+product picks wrong grade variant. Action: defer to a follow-on prompt-tuning + Stage-A normalization iteration after the rest of Phase I lands. The eval test currently fails when thresholds are enforced — flag this for Task 17 deploy check; we may need to gate the eval test behind MATCHER_EVAL_STRICT=1 env var so CI doesn't fail on aspirational thresholds while iteration is in progress.
